pattern: Schoolbook addition digit by digit from the least-significant end, carrying as you go, without ever converting the whole string to a number.
hints: You cannot turn the strings into integers, so reproduce the addition you would do by hand on paper.
Align the two numbers at their right ends and add column by column, tracking a single carry.
Walk both strings from the back, sum the two digits plus the carry, append the units digit, keep the tens as the next carry, and reverse the result at the end.

edge_inputs.description: single digits producing a carry into a new column
both zero stays a single zero, no leading zeros introduced
carry propagates all the way and adds a leading digit
long chain of nines plus one, carry ripples across every column
very unequal lengths so most columns are single-sided

stress.description: two random large numbers without leading zeros
stress.note: first digit forced 1-9 to avoid leading zeros

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def addStrings(self, num1, num2):
        i, j = len(num1) - 1, len(num2) - 1
        carry = 0
        out = []
        while i >= 0 or j >= 0 or carry:
            a = ord(num1[i]) - 48 if i >= 0 else 0
            b = ord(num2[j]) - 48 if j >= 0 else 0
            s = a + b + carry
            out.append(chr(48 + s % 10))
            carry = s // 10
            i -= 1
            j -= 1
        return ''.join(reversed(out))
